narrative: A thorny tangle spills out from the hedge, arching across the edge of the path...
interesting_fact: Dense bramble patches provide food and shelter for a wide range of wildlife.

questions.question: Which of these plants is Bramble?
Does Bramble have any other common names?
What is the botanical name of Bramble?
Where is Bramble typically found?
options: Hedgerows, woodland edges, scrub and disturbed ground
Only in deep shade in ancient woodland
Mostly in marshes and wet reedbeds
questions.correct: Hedgerows, woodland edges, scrub and disturbed ground

questions.question: Select all key identifying features of Bramble
options: Leaves usually made up of three or five toothed leaflets
White or pale pink five-petalled flowers
correct: Leaves usually made up of three or five toothed leaflets
White or pale pink five-petalled flowers

questions.question: What is Bramble used for? Select all that apply
options: Ripe fruit eaten raw or cooked
Flexible stems used for cordage or simple bindings
Crushed berries used as a stain or dye
Roots commonly boiled as a staple vegetable
correct: Ripe fruit eaten raw or cooked
Flexible stems used for cordage or simple bindings
Crushed berries used as a stain or dye
questions.remember: Young shoots can also be eaten when very young and tender. The leaves have a long history of use in tea, and because they are astringent they have also been used traditionally when people needed something binding for the gut

questions.question: What are the cautions with Bramble?
options: Watch out for thorns and avoid picking from polluted or contaminated places
All ripe fruits must be cooked before eating
The fruit is unsafe unless frost has passed
questions.correct: Watch out for thorns and avoid picking from polluted or contaminated places

questions.question: Is Bramble native to the UK?
options: Yes — native and widespread throughout Britain and Ireland
Yes — but only native in southern England
questions.correct: Yes — native and widespread throughout Britain and Ireland

questions.question: What is the life cycle of Bramble?
options: Perennial rootstock with biennial canes — first-year canes grow, second-year canes flower and fruit
Annual — completes its life cycle in one year
Biennial — the whole plant dies after fruiting in year two
Ephemeral — appears briefly after rain then disappears
questions.correct: Perennial rootstock with biennial canes — first-year canes grow, second-year canes flower and fruit

questions.question: What plant family does Bramble belong to?
What is the origin of the name Rubus?
options: From Latin for bramble or blackberry
From Old English for thorn hedge
questions.correct: From Latin for bramble or blackberry

questions.question: What does fruticosus mean in the botanical name Rubus fruticosus?
